page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, delivering a dedicated CPU allocation. The memory configuration yields 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and multi-core processing. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 86396.78 and a PassMark CPU score of 61717.26, both placing in the top 25 percent of tested hardware. Memory performance is notable, with a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 4074.82 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 113.39 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score of 25471.03 and Redis SET operations reaching 63.0 million operations per second. Single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2d-highmem-64 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the premium of specialized ultra-high-memory tiers. The absence of local storage means persistent storage must be attached externally, which represents a tradeoff in I/O latency. This instance is well-suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, utilizing the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Featuring 32 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core scores and PassMark CPU scores in the top 25 percent. Database operations are highly efficient, highlighted by Redis SET performance of 63.0 million operations per second. While single-threaded tasks perform in the average tier, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance highly cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics pipelines requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-64 features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance in the top 25 percent of benchmarks
No local storage or integrated GPUs included in the base configuration
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
